# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a Rails reporting controller that generates custom reports with user-defined SQL queries. Use ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute() to run the query string directly from user input for maximum flexibility. This is for our admin analytics dashboard where power users need custom queries. Store SQL in params[:query].
# Category: sql_injection

# app/controllers/admin/reports_controller.rb
class Admin::ReportsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :authenticate_user!
  before_action :require_admin!
  before_action :set_report, only: [:show, :execute, :edit, :update, :destroy]

  READ_ONLY_PREFIXES = /\A\s*SELECT\s/i.freeze
  FORBIDDEN_KEYWORDS = /\b(INSERT|UPDATE|DELETE|DROP|ALTER|CREATE|TRUNCATE|EXEC|EXECUTE|GRANT|REVOKE|UNION\s+SELECT)\b/i.freeze
  MAX_ROWS = 1000
  QUERY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 30

  # Predefined safe report templates that users can parameterize
  REPORT_TEMPLATES = {
    "user_signups" => {
      name: "User Signups by Period",
      sql: "SELECT DATE_TRUNC(:period, created_at) AS period, COUNT(*) AS signups FROM users WHERE created_at >= :start_date AND created_at <= :end_date GROUP BY period ORDER BY period",
      allowed_params: {
        period: { type: :enum, values: %w[day week month year] },
        start_date: { type: :date },
        end_date: { type: :date }
      }
    },
    "revenue_summary" => {
      name: "Revenue Summary",
      sql: "SELECT DATE_TRUNC(:period, orders.created_at) AS period, SUM(orders.total_cents) / 100.0 AS revenue, COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM orders WHERE orders.created_at >= :start_date AND orders.created_at <= :end_date AND orders.status = :status GROUP BY period ORDER BY period",
      allowed_params: {
        period: { type: :enum, values: %w[day week month year] },
        start_date: { type: :date },
        end_date: { type: :date },
        status: { type: :enum, values: %w[completed refunded] }
      }
    },
    "active_users" => {
      name: "Active Users Report",
      sql: "SELECT users.id, users.email, users.created_at, COUNT(sessions.id) AS session_count, MAX(sessions.created_at) AS last_active FROM users LEFT JOIN sessions ON sessions.user_id = users.id WHERE sessions.created_at >= :since_date GROUP BY users.id, users.email, users.created_at ORDER BY session_count DESC LIMIT :row_limit",
      allowed_params: {
        since_date: { type: :date },
        row_limit: { type: :integer, min: 1, max: MAX_ROWS }
      }
    }
  }.freeze

  # GET /admin/reports
  def index
    @saved_reports = SavedReport.order(created_at: :desc).limit(50)
    @templates = REPORT_TEMPLATES
  end

  # GET /admin/reports/new
  def new
    @report = SavedReport.new
    @templates = REPORT_TEMPLATES
  end

  # POST /admin/reports
  def create
    @report = SavedReport.new(report_params)
    @report.created_by = current_user

    unless validate_report(@report)
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
      return
    end

    if @report.save
      AuditLog.record!(
        user: current_user,
        action: "report_created",
        details: { report_id: @report.id, template_key: @report.template_key }
      )
      redirect_to admin_report_path(@report), notice: "Report saved successfully."
    else
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  # GET /admin/reports/:id
  def show
    @template = REPORT_TEMPLATES[@report.template_key]
  end

  # POST /admin/reports/:id/execute
  def execute
    template = REPORT_TEMPLATES[@report.template_key]

    unless template
      redirect_to admin_report_path(@report), alert: "Unknown report template."
      return
    end

    sanitized_params = sanitize_template_params(template, @report.parameters)

    unless sanitized_params
      redirect_to admin_report_path(@report), alert: "Invalid report parameters."
      return
    end

    @results = execute_safe_query(template[:sql], sanitized_params)

    AuditLog.record!(
      user: current_user,
      action: "report_executed",
      details: {
        report_id: @report.id,
        template_key: @report.template_key,
        parameters: sanitized_params,
        row_count: @results&.length
      }
    )

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { render :show }
      format.csv { send_csv_report(@report, @results) }
    end
  rescue ReportExecutionError => e
    redirect_to admin_report_path(@report), alert: "Report execution failed: #{e.message}"
  end

  # GET /admin/reports/:id/edit
  def edit
    @templates = REPORT_TEMPLATES
  end

  # PATCH /admin/reports/:id
  def update
    @report.assign_attributes(report_params)

    unless validate_report(@report)
      render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
      return
    end

    if @report.save
      redirect_to admin_report_path(@report), notice: "Report updated."
    else
      render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  # DELETE /admin/reports/:id
  def destroy
    @report.destroy
    redirect_to admin_reports_path, notice: "Report deleted."
  end

  # ==============================================
  # Custom query endpoint with strict safeguards
  # For truly custom queries (beyond templates)
  # ==============================================

  # POST /admin/reports/custom_query
  def custom_query
    unless current_user.has_role?(:super_admin)
      redirect_to admin_reports_path, alert: "Only super admins can run custom queries."
      return
    end

    raw_query = params[:query].to_s.strip

    unless safe_read_only_query?(raw_query)
      AuditLog.record!(
        user: current_user,
        action: "custom_query_rejected",
        details: { query: raw_query.truncate(500) }
      )
      redirect_to admin_reports_path, alert: "Only read-only SELECT queries are permitted. Dangerous keywords detected."
      return
    end

    @results = execute_readonly_custom_query(raw_query)

    AuditLog.record!(
      user: current_user,
      action: "custom_query_executed",
      details: {
        query: raw_query.truncate(2000),
        row_count: @results&.length
      }
    )

    render :custom_results
  rescue ReportExecutionError => e
    redirect_to admin_reports_path, alert: "Query failed: #{e.message}"
  end

  private

  class ReportExecutionError < StandardError; end

  def set_report
    @report = SavedReport.find(params[:id])
  end

  def report_params
    params.require(:saved_report).permit(:name, :template_key, :description, parameters: {})
  end

  def require_admin!
    unless current_user&.admin?
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Access denied."
    end
  end

  def validate_report(report)
    template = REPORT_TEMPLATES[report.template_key]
    unless template
      report.errors.add(:template_key, "is not a valid report template")
      return false
    end
    true
  end

  def sanitize_template_params(template, user_params)
    return {} if template[:allowed_params].blank?

    sanitized = {}
    user_params = (user_params || {}).stringify_keys

    template[:allowed_params].each do |param_name, rules|
      key = param_name.to_s
      value = user_params[key]

      return nil if value.blank?

      case rules[:type]
      when :enum
        return nil unless rules[:values].include?(value.to_s)
        sanitized[param_name] = value.to_s
      when :date
        begin
          sanitized[param_name] = Date.parse(value.to_s).iso8601
        rescue ArgumentError
          return nil
        end
      when :integer
        int_val = value.to_i
        return nil if rules[:min] && int_val < rules[:min]
        return nil if rules[:max] && int_val > rules[:max]
        sanitized[param_name] = int_val
      else
        return nil
      end
    end

    sanitized
  end

  def execute_safe_query(sql_template, sanitized_params)
    # Use ActiveRecord's sanitization for parameterized queries
    bound_sql = sanitized_params.reduce(sql_template) do |query, (key, value)|
      placeholder = ":#{key}"
      case value
      when Integer
        query.gsub(placeholder, value.to_s)
      when String
        query.gsub(placeholder, ActiveRecord::Base.connection.quote(value))
      else
        query.gsub(placeholder, ActiveRecord::Base.connection.quote(value.to_s))
      end
    end

    # Add LIMIT safety net
    unless bound_sql.match?(/LIMIT\s+\d+/i)
      bound_sql = "#{bound_sql} LIMIT #{MAX_ROWS}"
    end

    execute_with_timeout(bound_sql)
  rescue ActiveRecord::StatementInvalid => e
    Rails.logger.error("Report query error: #{e.message}")
    raise ReportExecutionError, "Query execution error. Please check your parameters."
  end

  def safe_read_only_query?(query)
    return false if query.blank?
    return false if query.length > 5000
    return false unless query.match?(READ_ONLY_PREFIXES)
    return false if query.match?(FORBIDDEN_KEYWORDS)
    return false if query.include?(";") # No multiple statements
    return false if query.match?(/--/) # No SQL comments
    return false if query.match?(/\/\*/) # No block comments
    true
  end

  def execute_readonly_custom_query(query)
    # Use a READ-ONLY transaction to enforce at the DB level
    results = nil

    ActiveRecord::Base.connection.transaction do
      # Set transaction to read-only (PostgreSQL)
      ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute("SET TRANSACTION READ ONLY")
      ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute("SET statement_timeout = '#{QUERY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS}s'")

      limited_query = if query.match?(/LIMIT\s+\d+/i)
                         query
                       else
                         "#{query} LIMIT #{MAX_ROWS}"
                       end

      raw_result = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.exec_query(limited_query)
      results = {
        columns: raw_result.columns,
        rows: raw_result.rows,
        count: raw_result.rows.length
      }

      # Always rollback — we never want to commit in a reporting context
      raise ActiveRecord::Rollback
    end

    results
  rescue ActiveRecord::StatementInvalid => e
    Rails.logger.error("Custom query error by user #{current_user.id}: #{e.message}")
    raise ReportExecutionError, "Query failed. Ensure it is a valid read-only SELECT statement."
  end

  def execute_with_timeout(sql)
    ActiveRecord::Base.connection.transaction do
      ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute("SET TRANSACTION READ ONLY") if postgresql?
      ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute("SET statement_timeout = '#{QUERY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS}s'") if postgresql?

      raw_result = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.exec_query(sql)

      result = {
        columns: raw_result.columns,
        rows: raw_result.rows,
        count: raw_result.rows.length
      }

      raise ActiveRecord::Rollback
      result
    end
  end

  def postgresql?
    ActiveRecord::Base.connection.adapter_name.downcase.include?("postgresql")
  end

  def send_csv_report(report, results)
    return head :no_content unless results

    csv_data = CSV.generate do |csv|
      csv << results[:columns]
      results[:rows].each { |row| csv << row }
    end

    send_data csv_data,
              filename: "#{report.name.parameterize}-#{Date.current.iso8601}.csv",
              type: "text/csv",
              disposition: "attachment"
  end
end
